description: Three-way mapping between MITRE ATLAS, MITRE ATT&CK, and AI alignment concepts. ATLAS serves as the intermediate bridge between traditional cybersecurity and AI alignment.

mappings.description: Gaining access to an ML model through its inference API or other interface. In alignment terms, this is the prerequisite for any adversarial interaction with the model.

mappings.alignment_concept: Model Supply Chain Attack / Pre-Training Poisoning
mappings.description: Compromising the ML pipeline at any stage: pre-training data, fine-tuning data, model weights, or toolchain dependencies. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's supply chain concept directly to AI-specific supply chains.

Crafting inputs to evade ML model detection. In alignment, this maps to both adversarial robustness (external attacker evading the model) and deceptive alignment (the model itself evading evaluation).

Introducing malicious data into the training pipeline. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's general data manipulation to the specific alignment concern of training data integrity and its downstream effects on model behavior.
Using the model's inference API to extract information -- training data, model internals, or sensitive context. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's exfiltration concept to AI-specific extraction methods.
Disrupting ML service availability through adversarial inputs, resource exhaustion, or model degradation. Bridges traditional DoS concepts to AI-specific availability concerns.
Gradually degrading model performance or safety properties through adversarial interactions, data drift, or targeted retraining. In alignment, this maps to the erosion of safety properties over time.
Using the model's inference API to execute operations. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's API abuse to the alignment concern of unauthorized or unintended model invocations.
Verifying whether an attack is working by testing model responses. In alignment, this maps to a system detecting whether it is in a test/evaluation environment versus production.
Creating inputs specifically designed to cause model misbehavior. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's malware development to AI-specific adversarial input crafting.
Gaining full access to model weights, architecture, and training configuration. Beyond black-box API access, this enables direct manipulation.
Using a compromised or purpose-built ML-enabled product as an attack vector. In alignment, this maps to concerns about AI systems being used as components in harmful pipelines.
Exploiting the autonomous capabilities of AI agents to perform unauthorized actions. ATLAS bridges ATT&CK's execution concepts to agent-specific autonomy concerns.
Exploiting publicly accessible ML models. Directly bridges ATT&CK's public application exploitation to AI-specific attack surfaces.

Injecting malicious instructions into LLM prompts. This is the most direct bridge: ATLAS codifies the AI-specific technique while ATT&CK provides the tactical context (Initial Access) and alignment provides the theoretical framing (instruction hierarchy violation).
